channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: On board with Senegal’s navy as it searches for migrants on a popular but deadly route toward Europe
item.description: DAKAR, Senegal (AP) — As dawn arrived off the coast of Senegal, navy commander Assane Seye scanned the horizon.
item.dc:creator: ANNIE RISEMBERG, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Middle East latest: US envoy holds talks in Lebanon on Israel-Hezbollah cease-fire proposal
item.description: U.S. envoy Amos Hochstein held talks with Lebanese officials in Beirut on Tuesday about a possible cease-fire in the Israel-Hezbollah war. Hochstein, a senior advisor to U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Croatia to hold a presidential election on Dec. 29
item.description: ZAGREB, Croatia (AP) — Croatia will hold a presidential election on Dec. 29, Prime Minister Andrej Plenković announced Tuesday. The government is expected on Thursday to formally schedule the vote. If no candidate wins an outright majority on Dec. 29, a runoff vote will take place two weeks later.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Sweden, Finland and Norway release new advice on surviving war amid concerns over Russia, Ukraine escalation
item.description: Sweden, Finland and Norway this week released detailed guidance for citizens on how to survive a crisis situation like extreme weather or war-like conditions that could hamper food or internet access.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US recognizes Venezuela's opposition candidate as president-elect months after the disputed election
item.description: The U.S. government has recognized Venezuelan opposition candidate Edmundo González as the “president-elect” of the South American country, months after President Nicolas Maduro claimed to have won the July contest.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: 1,000 days of war in Ukraine as Zelenskyy doubles down on aerial options with ATACMS, drones and missiles
item.description: Ukraine marks 1,000 days of war with Russia on Tuesday as President Zelenskyy eyes plan to develop thousands of long-range drones and military targets inside Russia using US-supplied ATACMS.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Putin lowers threshold for Russian nuclear weapons use after Biden's Ukraine missile decision
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin on Tuesday lowered the threshold for nuclear weapons use, just two days after the U.S. authorized Ukraine to use long-range missiles inside of Russia.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: 45 pro-democracy activists get 4 to 10 years in prison in Hong Kong's biggest national security case
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — Forty-five ex-lawmakers and activists were sentenced to four to 10 years in prison Tuesday in Hong Kong’s biggest national security case under a Beijing-imposed law that crushed a once-thriving pro-democracy movement.
item.dc:creator: KANIS LEUNG and ZEN SOO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Concerns grow for imprisoned Nobel Peace Prize laureate Narges Mohammadi's health in Iran
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Imprisoned Nobel Peace Prize laureate Narges Mohammadi underwent a complex surgery in Iran that saw part of a bone in her right leg removed over cancer fears but was immediately returned to prison, raising the risks to her life, rights groups warned.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UK farmers plan to protest at Parliament over a tax hike they say will ruin family farms
item.description: LONDON (AP) — With banners, bullhorns, toy tractors and an angry message, British farmers are descending on Parliament on Tuesday to protest a hike in inheritance tax that they say will deal a “hammer blow” to struggling family farms. U.K.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden's Ukraine missile move will have 'some impact': Retired major
item.description: Lyons questioned the timing of the decision, saying, “I’m not sure why we didn’t allow this two or three years ago.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: Biden’s Ukraine move tests Putin, riles Trump allies
item.description: The Biden administration is apparently calling Russian leader Vladimir Putin’s bluff by allowing Ukraine to strike far into Russian territory with U.S. weapons systems, a move that has drawn the ire of President-elect Trump’s allies.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Thune threatens International Criminal Court with sanctions if it doesn’t drop Netanyahu warrant for arrest
item.description: Sen. John Thune said he will go after the International Criminal Court with sanctions once the GOP take the majority in the Senate come January if the court does not drop its case against Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump Jr.: Biden administration trying to cause ‘World War 3’ by helping Ukraine
item.description: Donald Trump Jr., the eldest son of President-elect Trump, claimed the Biden administration is trying to cause “World War 3” by helping Ukraine in its war with Russia.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: From the Amazon rainforest, Biden declares nobody can reverse US progress on clean energy
item.description: MANAUS, Brazil (AP) — Speaking from the Amazon rainforest, President Joe Biden declared Sunday that there’s no going back in America’s “clean energy revolution” even as the incoming Trump administration vows to spur fossil fuel production and scale back efforts against climate change.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, GABRIELA SÁ PESSOA and COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Biden has authorized Ukraine to use long-range missiles inside Russia: Reports
item.description: President Biden authorized Ukraine to use U.S.-supplied missiles to strike further into Russia, multiple reports said. The move comes as about 10,000 North Korean troops aid Russia in its war and expect to fight in Ukraine in the “next several weeks.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Trump compiles his pro-Israel team: Here are the key players
item.description: President-elect Trump is compiling one of the most pro-Israel teams of any administration in history, following a presidential battle that saw Vice President Harris lose support from voters upset the Biden administration armed Israel in its war against Hamas.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Cruise line offers 4-year cruise to 'skip forward,' in wake of U.S. election results
item.description: One cruise line is offering travelers a customized journey for up to four years, to "skip forward," in the wake of the recent presidential election.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Mexican president might be changing view on US as Trump win sends warning to ruling socialists
item.description: Following Trump’s victory, the Mexican government, led by President Claudia Sheinbaum, is considering reassessing some of its policies to navigate the anticipated challenges.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump looms large as Biden set to meet China's Xi during Latin America summits
item.description: President Biden is in Latin America for the APEC and G-20 summits, in what is likely his last trip as president. The back-to-back summits come as an impending second Trump presidency is near, which may rattle some world leaders.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel's warfare in Gaza 'consistent with genocide:' UN special committee
item.description: Israel's destruction of vital water, sanitation, and food systems has created a "lethal mix of crises," the report said.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, NewsNation

item.title: T-Mobile hacked in massive Chinese cyber-espionage: Wall St. Journal
item.description: It was part of a campaign to spy on the phones of high-value intelligence targets, the Journal reports.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation

item.title: Montana Tucker's Oct. 7 film showcases young victims' strength
item.description: Montana Tucker's film, "The Children of October 7," documents what some of the attacks' youngest victims went through.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation Staff, NewsNation

item.title: Ukrainian troops train for trench warfare near France's WWI battlefields
item.description: Around 2,000 Ukrainian soldiers are training in France's eastern Marne region, where German and French armies battled each other during World War I.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Iran told Biden administration it won't try to assassinate President-elect Trump: report
item.description: Iran reportedly gave the White House its promise that it will not attempt to kill Donald Trump just weeks before the Justice Department charged a murder-for-hire man in a scheme to kill the former president.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Protesters storm Putin-backed parliament in Georgia breakaway region
item.description: Protesters in the separatist region of Abkhazia, Georgia stormed the parliament building to demand the region's president step down and reject an investment agreement that could benefit wealthy Russians.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Alex Soros, Jared Kushner reportedly obsessed with Albania
item.description: Alex Soros, whose father is Hungarian, reportedly travels to the capital Tirana so often he has called it “my second home.
item.dc:creator: Paula Froelich, NewsNation

item.title: US government worker charged with leaking classified documents on Israel's plans to strike Iran
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A man who worked for the U.S. government has been charged with leaking classified information assessing Israel's earlier plans to attack Iran, according to court papers filed Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: New search for MH370 Malaysia Airlines flight could begin soon
item.description: Flight MH370 vanished on March 8, 2014, shortly after departing Kuala Lumpur for Beijing with 239 people aboard.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: US says it will not limit Israel arms transfers after some improvements in flow of aid to Gaza
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Biden administration said Tuesday that Israel has made some good but limited progress in increasing the flow of humanitarian aid to Gaza and will not limit arms transfers to Israel as it had threatened to a month ago if the situation had not improved.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, JULIA FRANKEL and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Church of England head Justin Welby resigns over handling of sex abuse scandal
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Archbishop of Canterbury Justin Welby, head of the Church of England and spiritual leader of the global Anglican Communion, resigned Tuesday after an investigation found that he failed to tell police about serial physical and sexual abuse by a volunteer at Christian summer camps as soo
item.dc:creator: DANICA KIRKA, Associated Press, NewsNation
